name: Bittersweet Chocolate Peanut Butter Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted butter; cut into pieces
3 large Eggs; lightly beaten, room temperature
1/2 cup All-purpose flour; finely chopped nuts
1/4 cup Unsalted butter; cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
Bittersweet Chocolate Glazed Peanut Butter Cake
Cake: Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter a 9-inch round baking pan and line with buttered parchment paper. Set aside.
Melt chocolate and butter in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring occasionally until melted. Add sugar and stir until sugar has melted and mixture is smooth. Remove from heat and cool. Stir often. Whisk eggs into the mixture one at a time, beating well after each addition. Fold in flour and chopped nuts until well mixed. Spread batter evenly in pan. Bake 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool on a rack for half an hour.
Invert the cake onto a rack. Peel off parchment. Leave to cool.
Glaze: In a medium saucepan over low heat, combine bittersweet chocolate, butter, corn syrup, and cream. Stir often until chocolate is melted. Cool for 10 minutes.
Topping: Combine peanut butter and water. Mix well.
To assemble the cake, spread the topping mixture over the cake to form a layer of peanut butter. Place a piece of waxed paper or parchment under the cooling rack where the cake is resting. Pour glaze over the cake. Let stand for 15 minutes. Using a spatula, apply any glaze that runs onto the paper back on top of the cake. Repeat until all glaze is on the cake. Press chopped peanuts around the top edge of the cake.
